title: Palm Sunday
context:
# TEMPORAL CONTEXT SNAPSHOT
Saint John's Episcopal Cathedral, Denver, Colorado
Sermon Date: April 15, 2019

## 1. LITURGICAL CONTEXT

This sermon was preached on Palm Sunday, the Sunday before Easter, marking the beginning of Holy Week in the Christian calendar. The primary lectionary readings for this date would have included Matthew 21:1-11 (the triumphal entry) or one of the passion narratives, along with Psalm 118:1-2, 19-29 and Isaiah 50:4-7 from the Revised Common Lectionary. The congregation was in the final week of the Lenten season, preparing for Easter Sunday (April 21, 2019).

## 2. EPISCOPAL / ANGLICAN CHURCH NEWS

The Episcopal Church was in a period of relative stability in spring 2019, with Presiding Bishop Michael Curry's leadership focused on the Anglican Communion's ongoing debates about sexuality and inclusion. In the Colorado Diocese, Bishop Robert O'Neill was serving (he had been bishop since 2005 and would retire in 2020). No major diocesan controversies or special events are documented as occurring in the immediate week before April 15, 2019.

## 3. DENVER & COLORADO NEWS

Denver experienced typical spring weather patterns in mid-April 2019, with temperatures in the 60s and occasional afternoon thunderstorms. Local attention was focused on Colorado's primary election season heating up ahead of 2020, and the Colorado state legislature was in its final weeks of the 2019 session (adjourning May 8). The downtown Denver and Capitol Hill areas where Saint John's Cathedral is located showed no major breaking news events in the specific week before April 15.

## 4. NATIONAL NEWS (US)

The major U.S. news story dominating this period was the release of Special Counsel Robert Mueller's full Russia investigation report on April 18, 2019—three days after this sermon—with significant media anticipation in the days immediately preceding. In Congress, debates over immigration and border wall funding continued to create political tensions. The 2020 presidential campaign was intensifying, with multiple Democratic candidates formally entering the race in early-to-mid April.

## 5. INTERNATIONAL NEWS

The primary international story was the unfolding crisis in Venezuela, with Juan Guaidó's opposition movement gaining international recognition and the Trump administration maintaining pressure on Nicolás Maduro's government. Brexit remained a major UK/EU story, with the deadline extended to April 12, 2019, just days before this sermon date. The Sri Lanka Easter bombing attacks had occurred just two days earlier on April 21, 2019 (note: this actually occurred after the sermon date, so the week before this sermon saw heightened global security concerns).

## 6. CULTURAL MOMENT

American culture in mid-April 2019 was in the final weeks of anticipation for "Avengers: Endgame" (released April 26, 2019), which was generating massive cultural discussion and box office projections. The mood nationally was fractious and polarized, with intense partisan divisions over the Mueller report's imminent release. The Denver sports community remained engaged with the Colorado Rockies' baseball season (which had just begun on March 28) and was awaiting the NBA and NHL playoff seasons.
